primary_accounting_issue: Éligibilité, dans les comptes consolidés, de la composante de risque de change d’une créance de dividende intragroupe comptabilisée comme élément couvert en comptabilité de couverture

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule that IFRS supersedes IAS for overlapping hedge-accounting eligibility and hedged-item questions, so IFRS 9 governs and IAS 39 is discarded.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low uncertainty: the issue is a hedge-accounting eligibility question for a hedged item in consolidated financial statements, and IFRS 9 directly addresses that area with overlapping provisions.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern what can be a hedged item in consolidated financial statements and identify the IFRS 9 hedge-accounting models.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs clarify how foreign exchange differences on intragroup monetary items behave in consolidated financial statements and therefore constrain hedged-item eligibility analysis.
This Interpretation clarifies the distinct hedge of a net investment model and the foreign currency risk that may be designated within a group.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs relate to hedge-accounting disclosures rather than identifying the governing hedge-accounting approaches.

treatment_families.family: Hedge-accounting models for foreign currency risk on a recognised or qualifying hedged item in consolidated financial statements

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a named top-level hedge-accounting model for exposures to changes in fair value of recognised assets or liabilities attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a named top-level hedge-accounting model for variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment as a distinct named hedge-accounting model for foreign currency risk within a group.
